OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

First, I try to limit my social media use. I know it's easy to get sucked in, but I've found that taking breaks helps me clear my head. Second, I've started fact-checking. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly a few times weekly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As someone who's struggled with depression, I've become increasingly aware of the impact of fake content on social media. It's not just annoying, it's overwhelming. With so many people sharing misinformation, it's hard to know what to believe. But I've found a few strategies that help me manage the noise. First, I try to limit my social media use. I know it's easy to get sucked in, but I've found that taking breaks helps me clear my head. Second, I've started fact-checking. I know it's not always easy to verify information, but there are some great resources out there that can help. Finally, I've tried to focus on real connections. Instead of following influencers or celebrities, I've started following friends and family. It's amazing how much more authentic and supportive the conversations are when you're interacting with people you know. Of course, it's not always easy. There are days when I feel like I'm drowning in a sea of fake news. But I've learned to take it one step at a time. One thing at a time. And slowly but surely, I'm figuring out how to navigate this crazy world of social media. I'm not naive, I know there's always going to be some level of deception. But I've learned to trust my instincts, to be skeptical, and to not take it all so seriously. And that's made all the difference for me.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I realized that social media is a reflection of our society, and that fake content is a symptom of a larger issue. I decided to take control of my own experience by limiting my use, fact-checking, and focusing on real connections. I also acknowledged that it's okay to not know everything, and that it's okay to ask for help. I'm not a tech expert, but I'm a human being trying to make sense of this world. And I've found that sometimes, the simplest solutions are the most effective.
